person.name: Desire Ntambiye
person.bio: DevOps Engineer with experience since 2020, contributing to impactful projects across multiple organizations. I have expertise with public cloud platforms including AWS, GCP, and Azure, as well as private cloud environments. I specialize in designing microservices architectures and building secure, resilient, and scalable Kubernetes-based infrastructures following DevOps best practices. My background includes two years working on retail applications and over two years delivering solutions for banking systems. I also have experience supporting machine learning projects, further strengthening my ability to work in complex, high-performance environments.

person.name: Ryan Nemeth
person.bio: I’m Ryan Nemeth, a seasoned DevOps Engineer with over 15 years of experience. I have a strong focus on cloud-native technologies and infrastructure automation, specializing in Kubernetes, Azure, and CI/CD pipeline development. I have deep experience with CNCF tools such as Prometheus, Helm, and Fluent Bit, and I also bring a solid background in software development using Go, C#, Python, Bash, and PowerShell. I’m passionate about knowledge sharing—whether through internal enablement sessions or publicly via my blog at rnemeth90.github.io.

person.name: Ilya Kozak
person.bio: System Engineer, diving deep into Linux, k8s, CI/CD automation, AWS, DevOps best practices.

person.name: Javan Zhao
person.bio: I'm an SRE engineer focused on DevOps, stability, and observability indicators, and I'm passionate about the Kubernetes ecosystem.

person.name: Kamil Aliev
person.bio: I began my journey in IT with the Python for Everybody course and have been fully immersed in this field ever since. The School 42 program provided me a solid foundation, and after trying out a little bit of everything, I found the DevOps space really fascinating. Here, I still engage in various  activities, such as CI/CD, platform engineering, and build engineering.  In my solutions, I prioritize simplicity and scalability, truly embracing the Unix philosophy of using simple tools that excel at a single task. That’s also why I love Vim, and you should too!  I enjoy learning and becoming more efficient in both my job and daily life, and programs like Kubestronaut definitely add some color to my journey!

person.name: Luis Schweigard
person.bio: I am a Cloud Platform Engineer who enjoys working with anything CNCF related. My specialties include Kubernetes and Observability. In the CNCF, I have held a talk at the official CNCF Meetup in Hamburg @ MaibornWolff about KEDA Autoscaling and assisted in other talks aswell.

person.name: Mario Fahlandt

He is working as a Customer Delivery Architect with the focus on planning and building concepts and architecture for Infrastructure in the cloud native world. Furthermore, he started the Google Developer Group for Cloud in Munich back in 2016. His passion is always to move the cloud native community one step forward. He is part of the Kubernetes SIG ContribEx and helps in the SIG K8s Infra with reporting.

person.name: Simon Rupar
person.bio: Platform Engineer passionate about Kubernetes, automation, and resiliency. Public speaker, mentor, and community organizer with Kubernetes Slovenia Meetup.

person.name: Akilan Subramanian

I’m also passionate about teaching container technologies to beginners and helping others grow in their engineering journey.

person.name: Edik Mkoyan
person.bio: I run the infraheads DevOps agency where I also lead the development of our turnkey Kubernetes cluster management toolset called turnk8s showcased on awesome-talos repository. I blog mostly here https://medium.com/@info_98249In the early 2020s, I actively advanced the DevOps movement in Armenia while overseeing Cloud and DevOps at EPAM Armenia. My leadership in DevOps engineering and abiding interest in AI led me to MLOps and now we partner with Netris, DVC and Outbound for OnPrem AI infrastructure deployment and management.

person.bio: A passionated solution builder enjoys turning innovative ideas into real-world solutions; growing PoC cases into Enterprise production environments solving challenging questions. person.name: Kannan N

My expertise as a DevOps engineer and cloud architect allows me to design and manage scalable, resilient, and efficient digital ecosystems. With a deep mastery of Kubernetes, I’ve helped organizations streamline operations and optimize cloud-native infrastructure. My passion for automation and cloud technologies drives me to constantly improve system performance and ensure high availability.

person.name: Hajnal Máté
person.bio: Hey! It’s a cool jacket.

Passionate snowboarder, DIY and semi-professional BBQ Pitmaster and baker!Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Being ambassador makes your part of the most fantastic crowd in the Tech World these days. I deeply enjoy diving into the latest technological trends and translating these insights into internal technical sharing to foster a strong DevOps culture within my team. I look forward to continuously embracing new challenges in this fast-paced tech landscape.
person.location: Sanchong District, New Taipei City, Taiwan

I specialize in modern cloud architectures and microservices, helping organizations move from legacy or fragmented systems into resilient, observable, and secure cloud-native platforms. My background blends infrastructure engineering, cloud security, and platform architecture, allowing me to design solutions that are not only scalable-but defensible, cost-aware, and production-ready. Outside of work, you'll usually find me indoor rock climbing or fueling up with a meticulously brewed cup of coffee.

Skilled in bridging security, automation, and regulatory requirements to deliver resilient and scalable solutions.

person.bio: I am an engineer at a Singapore-based IT solutions provider, and when I'm not solving tech problems, I'm either potting balls in cue sports or achieving a high KDA ratio in PC gaming.

person.name: Kashyap Savaliya
person.bio: Kash is a tech-savvy tinkerer who loves building out his homelab and automating every corner of his home — if it can be smart, he'll make it smart. When he's not knee-deep in servers and scripts, you'll find him out on a run or grinding through a calisthenics session. He's the kind of person who's always leveling up, whether it's his fitness or his home network. Equal parts geek and athlete, Kash lives by the motto that everything can be optimized.

I have also explored other CNCF projects such as Istio, Prometheus, Grafana, Keycloak, cert-manager, etc for building internal platforms for design teams to build robust solutions.

person.name: Philipp Schmitt
person.bio: Philipp is a Platform engineer and technical trainer at inovex. Interested in optimizing software architecture and reducing cognitive load. Many years of experience in Kubernetes, Service Mesh, Observability and other CNCF technologies.

person.name: Michiel Peene

His R&D efforts focus on Cloud Network Automation, Management & Orchestration, and the Observability of Cloud Native environments.  Piotr has shared his knowledge at numerous industry conferences, including a presentation at Kubecon + CloudNativeCon Edge Day in 2023.  He is passionate about Kubernetes and its open-source ecosystem.

person.name: Ramiro Alvarez Fernandez
person.bio: Ramiro is a Senior DevOps Engineer with extensive experience in cloud infrastructure, and engineering leadership with solid experience in technologies such as Kubernetes, Elasticsearch, MongoDB. Ramiro's day-to-day involves managing Kubernetes clusters, orchestrating CI/CD pipelines, and overseeing Elasticsearch and MongoDB environments, all while pushing the limits of AWS, GCP, and Azure.  Ramiro is deeply passionate about Cloud FinOps, Observability, and optimizing cloud resource efficiency. Ramiro actively contribute to the cloud native community by sharing insights through presentations at conferences and wrote articles on different sites.

person.name: Dongho Han
person.bio: As a Cloud Architect at Samsung SDS, I design PaaS products like API Gateways. My background includes Java backend development and extensive experience in various SI projects. With my passion for cloud-native technologies and diverse skill set, I'm eager to contribute to various CNCF projects. I look forward to leveraging my experience and expertise to foster innovation and collaboration within the CNCF ecosystem, aiming to make meaningful contributions across multiple areas.

person.name: Irakli Ghvinianidze
person.bio: Just Ops

Well, I hope to help distribute it.

person.name: Barbara Téglás

He works on advanced penetration testing and security assessments for modern cloud-native environments, with a strong focus on reducing false positives through real-world exploitation and impact-driven reporting. Arno has hands-on expertise with CNCF technologies, container security, and automation in large-scale scanning and monitoring systems. He is passionate about helping organizations build secure and resilient platforms as they adopt Kubernetes and cloud-native architectures.

I look forward to participating in future CNCF meetups and taking a more active role in the community, sharing knowledge and collaborating with fellow cloud-native enthusiasts.

person.bio: I'm passionate about CloudNative Technologies, particularly interested in Kubernetes and the CNCF ecosystem. I like to share and talk about real-world scenarios, with a focus on optimizing performance and scalability.

person.name: Jelle
person.bio: System Engineer at a large government organization. I do enjoy working in a team but don't mind working alone. I'm all for quality of the products and services we deliver. Even though you won't find me on any socials I'm a pretty social guy :-)

person.name: Utkarsh Pundir
person.bio: Associate Solutions Architect @ AWS

person.name: Mariam Ben Ali

I’m proud to be a Kubeastronaut, constantly pushing the boundaries of what I can achieve in cloud, containerization, and monitoring. From handling complex systems to diving into microservices architecture, I’m always looking for the next challenge to grow my skills and contribute to the ever-evolving cloud-native landscape.

I have a strong interest in cutting-edge and emerging cloud-native technologies. My work focuses on CNCF-aligned tools, GitOps, CI/CD automation, infrastructure as code, and building reliable, scalable, and secure production systems

person.name: Lukas Panhirsch
person.bio: I'm a system administrator and software engineer at SenseLabs, specializing in self-hosted Kubernetes clusters using technologies like Talos Linux. Currently also studying Computer Engineering at the RWTH Aachen University in Germany.

person.name: Paul Verhoeven
person.bio: Hi!! 👋  I’m a Kubernetes/Platform engineer with a soft spot for logic and a healthy dose of curiosity. I like to know exactly how things tick — from the big picture down to the last YAML file. From setting up clusters to fine-tuning PostgreSQL and Kafka operators, I like being involved in every part of the stack.  Tech should make sense — and if it doesn’t, I’ll dig until it does (preferably with good coffee and a whiteboard nearby).

person.name: Benjamin Muschko
person.bio: Benjamin Muschko is a software engineer, consultant, and trainer with more than 20 years of experience in the industry. He specializes in cloud-native application development and transformation, container solutions, DevSecOps, and Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery implementations. Ben is an author, a frequent speaker at conferences, and an avid open source advocate. Among his published books are the Certified Kubernetes Application Developer (CKAD) Study Guide, the Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) Study Guide, and the Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist (CKS) Study Guide.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am excited to teach beginners the basics of Kubernetes in a practical and relatable fashion. One way to learn Kubernetes effectively is by following the curriculum of the official Kubernetes certification exams. I will continue to produce hands-on learning resources in the form of written, and interactive media.

person.name: Lacina Zina
person.bio: Principal Container Infrastructure Engineer with a deep focus on Kubernetes and cloud-native platforms. I’ve architected and operated large-scale multi-tenant and single-tenant clusters, building reliable service mesh, networking, and GPU-accelerated systems across dozens of environments. My passion lies in simplifying complex systems, improving platform reliability, and creating strong engineering foundations through mentoring and architectural guidance.

person.name: Biswajit Nanda
person.bio: Engineering leader with more than 21 years of industry experience in multiple fields like DevOps/Site-Reliability Engineering, Multi-Cloud Operations, Platform Engineering, Multi-Cloud Solution Architecture, Observability & Customer Engineering. Sam holds advanced certifications such as VCDX7-CMA (#204) and VCIX-NV, underscoring his expertise in the field. He is also an active blogger at definit.co.uk and maintains a presence on Twitter as @sammcgeown.

person.bio: Started as a System Administrator with a strong focus on Linux and Open Source tools. During the time rolled into the DevOps culture and started learning about Containers, Kubernetes. At my current role my learning path is still growing. Ready to explore some new tools...

I am pursuing deeper involvement with the Cloud Native Computing Foundation ecosystem by mentoring engineers and contributing to real-world Kubernetes enablement initiatives.

person.name: Abdullah Sowilah
person.bio: DevOps engineer focused on modernizing infrastructure and platform tooling. My specialties include Kubernetes, GitOps with ArgoCD, Helm, Terraform, and CNCF observability tooling. I design self-healing clusters, automate secure workload delivery, and strive to reduce developer cognitive load so teams ship faster and safer.

person.name: Shailendra Singh Chauhan
person.bio: Cloud Solution Architect with experience in building and deploying cloud native applications. And yes a Kubernetes enthusiastic.

person.name: Simon Forster
person.bio: Simon Forster is a cloud native technology architect and engineer based in London. Simon has extensive experience working in heavily regulated financial institutions on the design, delivery and security of critical cloud native applications. He has a specific focus on ensuring cloud native technologies deliver against business goals. As co-chair of the CNCF Cartografos Group, Simon developed and co-authored the CNCF Cloud Native Maturity Model and the CNCF children’s book “Admiral Bash’s Island Adventure”. He has contributed to both the Business Value Subcommittee and Financial Services User Group.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: As a longtime member of the cloud native community, I’m excited to evangelise its mission. I look forward to mentoring and training members of the community, no matter the technical skill set, to better understand why cloud native serves digital innovation and meets business goals. I strongly believe that every person in the community can help drive cloud native forward and am excited to help achieve this goal.

person.name: Alper Devrim
person.bio: DevOps Engineer with expertise in modern cloud-native technologies, including Kubernetes, Helm, and Prometheus. Experienced in automating deployments, optimizing infrastructure, and ensuring scalable, reliable, and secure systems. Passionate about CI/CD pipelines, monitoring solutions, and improving operational efficiency.

person.name: Ozigre Diakpa Brice-Arnaud
person.bio: System Administrator, DevSecOps

person.name: Sunny Keila
person.bio: I am a DevOps Engineer with extensive experience in AWS cloud-native development and Kubernetes orchestration, specializing in building scalable, resilient infrastructure solutions. My expertise spans across CNCF technologies including Kubernetes, Helm, Prometheus, and service mesh implementations, with a strong focus on CI/CD pipeline optimization and infrastructure as code. I'm passionate about helping organizations modernize their infrastructure and adopt cloud-native practices for improved scalability and operational efficiency.

person.name: Atif Mehmood Malik
person.bio: Competent and highly experienced Cloud Architect with more than 12 years of experience in designing scalable, secure, and performance optimized Cloud infrastructure platforms. Well versed in Cloud Native technologies like Docker, Kubernetes, Ansible, Terraform, Argo, and Flux. Expertise in designing, deploying, and managing infrastructure for business-critical applications and workloads in AWS, Azure, and OCI.Led the digital transformation journey across multiple large UAE Federal Government entities and national projects by playing key role as Cloud / Infrastructure Architect.

person.name: Nguyen Tien Dat
person.bio: I'm a DevSecOps Engineer in Ha Noi, specializing in designing and optimizing systems that require high availability and large-scale scalability. Through deep research into Kubernetes, I discovered the CNCF, where I’ve gained valuable knowledge and practical insights into cloud-native technologies. I’m always excited about connecting with experts - not only to exchange professional experience, but also to share perspectives on life beyond work.

person.name: Naga Chakradhar Ayyapu
person.bio: I’m an SRE/Platform/DevOps Engineer with around 8 years of experience in building and maintaining cloud-native platforms. My expertise lies in managing Kubernetes workloads, automating infrastructure, and improving platform reliability. I’m passionate about exploring new Cloud Native technologies and applying them to create efficient, scalable, and resilient systems.

person.name: Alessandro Vozza
person.bio: Community leader and CNCF ambassador, Alessandro has spent the last few years building cloud native infrastructures for Microsoft customers, animating the Dutch community, and training others to pass the CKx exams. He has passion for all things cloud native, he's been around open source for 25 years and recently founded a startup that offers innovative Namespaces-as-a-service for developers. Twitter handle: @bongoWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: Because I was before and it was awesome!

person.name: Ahmed Saad Hassan
person.bio: I'm Ahmed Saad, a passionate DevOps Engineer with strong expertise in Kubernetes (CKA, CKS, KCNA certified), CI/CD, and infrastructure automation using Terraform, Ansible, and Helm. I work extensively with cloud-native and container security tools like Trivy, Grype, and OPA, and manage scalable infrastructure on AWS and GCP. I'm currently expanding my skills into DevSecOps, with a focus on secure software delivery pipelines. I actively share knowledge through blog: ahmedsaadx.github.com and GitHub projects at github.com/ahmedsaadx. I'm also involved in the local DevOps and CNCF community through events and Meetups

person.name: Matt Bargenquast
person.bio: Matt discovered his passion for Kubernetes as a software engineer while migrating systems to a cloud-native architecture. This ignited a career journey that led him to become a Principal Site Reliability Engineer and a strong advocate for building scalable, observable, and reliable software systems.

person.name: Mouhamad Koreissi
person.bio: An experienced Sales Engineer | Solutions Architect currently working at Datadog, I have extensive expertise in delivering complex software to small, medium and large organisations using MEDDICC.  My customers have ranged from start-up and mid-market eCommerce companies to French CAC40 organisations in travel industry, financial services and transport.  With a strong interest in Cloud communities, I've been co-leading the Bamako AWS User Group for the past 2 years.  Check out my website to say hi: https://koreissi.com

person.name: Maxim Filipovich
person.bio: I’m a Site Reliability Engineer, passionate about Kubernetes, cloud-native infrastructure, everything Linux-related, and open source. I love being on the cutting edge of technology and bringing things together that weren’t originally meant to be together. I am also fully AWS certified and a holder of the “Gold Jacket.”

person.name: Filip Wardzichowski
person.bio: Senior Cloud & Network Architect with extensive experience in data center and service provider networking, specializing in cloud-native networking and security. Expertise includes Kubernetes networking (CNI), service meshes, eBPF, SDN, EVPN, and workload segmentation. Strong focus on integrating OpenShift and OpenStack with data center fabrics to build scalable and secure private cloud solutions. An advocate for DevOps and GitOps methodologies, driving automation, declarative infrastructure, and continuous integration in cloud-native environments. Currently part of Cisco’s Cloud Networking Business Unit, contributing to the development of Kubernetes and cloud-native networking solutions.

person.name: Yu Jie Zheng
person.bio: Kubernetes friend, cloud-native nerd, conference wanderer, perpetual learner, challenge chaser, and part-time failed trader.  https://yujie70338.github.io/

person.name: Marius Tanawa Tsamo
person.bio: I have a Master's degree in Systems, Network, and Security. Although I'm very passionate about container environments, I'm even more passionate about meaningful contributions. I'm familiar with most of the graduated projects in the CNCF landscape. ArgoCD, Istio, Cilium ect..

person.name: Jackie Virgo
person.bio: I am a DevOps engineer specializing in cloud infrastructure and Kubernetes. I focus on designing, automating, and operating scalable, secure platforms using modern DevOps and GitOps practices. My work centers around CNCF technologies such as Kubernetes, Helm, Terraform, and cloud-native observability and security tooling. I enjoy simplifying complex systems, improving developer experience, and building reliable infrastructure that supports production workloads.

person.name: Leonid Kopylov
person.bio: I am a technology enthusiast and AWS professional, holding all certifications in the field. I thrive on learning new things and am currently passionate about mastering Kubernetes. Outside of work, I enjoy exploring the latest tech trends.

person.name: Hyehun Lim
person.bio: Currently, I'm a Technical Account Manager at AWS for FSI and Edu-tech. As my customers have used lots of K8S projects for their production workload, I have been studying this area for a couple of years to support them. As a Kubestronaut, I still have new things to catch up and explorer even after this. I still have a long way to go.

person.name: Jannik Karb
person.bio: I’m a Cloud Native Engineer working on Kubernetes platform engineering and operations. I genuinely enjoy exploring new and interesting topics, then applying them to build better systems. My work typically involves container orchestration, automation/GitOps, and building reliable day-2 operations.

person.name: Eduard Shchudlo
person.bio: I’m a DevOps engineer focused on cloud-native platform engineering. I specialize in Kubernetes and CNCF ecosystem technologies, with strong experience in GitOps (Argo CD), CI/CD automation, and AWS environments. I build and maintain IaC using Terraform to deliver scalable, repeatable infrastructure. I enjoy improving reliability, performance, and developer experience through practical automation.

person.name: Jackson Ching
person.bio: I am an IT professional with over 20 years of experience specializing in Linux, digital forensics, and end-to-end solution design for enterprise environments. I have led teams in systems integration and mentored engineers, enhancing operational efficiency through cloud, virtualization, and automation expertise. My strategic approach consistently delivers high-quality, secure infrastructure solutions across diverse industries.

person.name: Serdar Köktaş
person.bio: I am a Platform Engineer focused on cloud infrastructure and Kubernetes, specializing in designing and managing scalable, secure, and efficient cloud-native environments.

person.name: Hoai Nguyen
person.bio: I am Hoai Nguyen, a DevOps engineer at AgileOps with strong experience in cloud-native technologies. I specialize in Kubernetes, AWS cloud services, and CNCF tools for building scalable and resilient systems. I focus on automation, performance optimization, and effective collaboration across Kubernetes, CNCF, and AWS ecosystems, helping organizations adopt modern DevOps practices effectively.

person.name: João Miguel Ribeiro Martins
person.bio: CNCF Enthusiast, Gitops & Platform Engineer

person.name: Yusuke Sasaki
person.bio: I am a part of community of the below org based on certifications I have obtained: ・PMI - PMP ・ISC - CISSP, CCSP ・ISACA - CISM, CISA, CRISC, CGEIT, CDPSE

person.name: Boris Bradić
person.bio: DevOps Engineer focused on Kubernetes, containers, networking, and security. Love to share my knowledge with others on their path to the Cloud. Part of the AWS Community Builders in the Containers category. Inspired to improve and elevate my technical knowledge.
person.location: Josipa bana Jelačića, Kiseljak, FBIH, Bosnia and Herzegovina

person.name: Matthew Cascio
person.bio: Digital product leader. Speaker, author, advisor. KDC organizer in Washington, DC and Medellín, Colombia. KCD fiscal sponsor through my company NāOM Lab.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Excited to promote CNCF projects, initiatives and culture through community events and meetups.

person.name: Douglas Ruyz
person.bio: I’m Douglas Ruyz, an IT Expert at Santander Bank and a cloud-native/Kubernetes platform engineer focused on operating and securing production-grade Kubernetes across AWS EKS, Azure AKS, and OpenShift. I work with policy and security (Kyverno/RBAC), observability (Prometheus/Grafana/Elasticsearch/Dynatrace), container registries (Harbor, ACR, ECR), and autoscaling—node autoscaling with Karpenter and workload autoscaling with KEDA; I’m also studying Python for Kubernetes automation. I’m currently studying GitOps with Argo CD, and I’m Red Hat certified (RHCSA, RHCE, Red Hat Certified Specialist in Containers, and Red Hat Certified OpenShift Administrator) with an infrastructure background in OpenStack and VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF).

person.name: Joeri Chappin
person.bio: I am a Cloud Platform Engineer with a strong focus on Kubernetes and Linux, particularly Kubernetes platforms and CI/CD infrastructure. I have experience designing, implementing, and operating cloud-native environments. I work closely with customers to provide practical solutions and guidance for implementing and securing their cloud-native infrastructure.

person.name: Sammy Cheung
person.bio: Sammy is a technology expert with over 30 years of experience in software development and systems architecture. He is an AWS Gold Jacket Ambassador, a multi-cloud certified all-star, and a Kubestronaut. Sammy is passionate about empowering people to upskill and accelerate business success in Cloud technology.

person.name: Heechul Kim
person.bio: As a senior full-stack developer with over 20 years of experience at Samsung SDS,  I am passionate about Kubernetes and the cloud native ecosystem.  In particular, I am very interested in building and optimizing DevOps environments for generative AI and RAG implementations.

person.name: Horace Li
person.bio: Horace is currently working as Director of CNCF Asia, supporting China's cloud native ecosystem and open source projects. Horace is also the Director of OpenInfra Asia. Previously, Horace worked in the Intel Open Source Technology Center for 13 years, as a technical account manager supporting open source community projects engagement in China, including Cloud Computing, DataCenter and Client Computing. Outside of work, Horace likes jogging, swimming, music, movies, fashion and art.

person.name: Andy Peng
person.bio: PNW local. Seasoned software product builder and technologist based in Seattle (WA). Expertise in large-scale distributed systems and cloud computing: AI, ML + Data, containers & virtualization, serverless compute, open-source. Diverse experience encompassing both people management and technical leadership, including cross-company collaborations. Community Lead of Cloud Native Seattle. Technical advisory board. Guest lecturer of University of Washington. Photographer, Music lover, have a taylor made in walnut, and I love Icewine. Homepage: https://pengandy.com/Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am thrilled to be joining the CNCF Ambassador program. As someone who is deeply passionate about building and promoting fundamental technologies, particularly in the realm of Cloud Native solutions, I feel grateful for the opportunity to deepen my connections with the community and share my knowledge and experience with open-source users around the globe. By being part of this program, I hope to make a broader impact and bring even more value to Linux Foundation and CNCF. Additionally, I am eager to learn more about the needs of users and help them understand how they can effectively use cloud native and container technology to solve their problems.

person.name: Elna Vogel
person.bio:
Elna is an Event Manager at The Linux Foundation, mainly working on CNCF events, and managing events with Chinese aspects.  

Elna has extensive track records leading cross-functional events globally. Prior to joining the Linux Foundation, Elna produced and managed corporate events for Fortune 500 investment banks in Hong Kong such as State Street, Goldman Sachs and BNY Mellon. Fluent in English, Mandarin and Cantonese, she works effortlessly across all regions, countries and cultures.

person.name: Berat Uyanik
person.bio: With 7+ years of experience in the tech industry, I have successfully led numerous digital transformation projects by leveraging modern cloud-native technologies. I possess deep expertise in the CNCF ecosystem, particularly in managing Kubernetes clusters and streamlining deployments with Helm.My focus is on ensuring system security and scalability while significantly reducing operational overhead through automation. I thrive on solving complex infrastructure challenges and enhancing system visibility through advanced Prometheus monitoring and automated pipelines.

person.name: Hamdi Khelil
person.bio: SRE / DevOps / Cloud Engineer specialized in Kubernetes platform management. Expert in orchestration, automation, and security. Possessing strong skills in creating, deploying, and optimizing clusters. I am passionate about innovation and constantly seeking ways to improve the performance and reliability of container-based infrastructures.

person.name: Eduard Tiron
person.bio: Cloud/DevOps/Software/Mechatronics Engineer with deep expertise in Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. Constantly seeking ways to improve my skills and deepen my knowledge in many rapidly evolving fields. More about me at https://etiron.netlify.app

person.name: Teguh Rais Rahmadi
person.bio: I am a DevOps Engineer with professional experience implementing microservices for SaaS applications and optimizing system infrastructure. I specialize in cloud server management, DevOps automation, DevSecOps practices, and designing cost-effective autoscaling strategies for Kubernetes clusters. I am interested in deepening my knowledge of cloud-native technologies to adapt and build more efficient and modern infrastructure.

person.name: Shao Wang
person.bio: I am a DevOps Engineer at Delta Electronics, focusing on managing our Kubernetes platforms. I really enjoy learning about cloud-native tools and solving technical challenges every day. I am excited to join this community and keep growing with other Kubernetes fans.

person.name: Vishal Anarase
person.bio: Software Developer @Civo | Contributor @Kubernetes @Percona | Cluster API Release team | Open Source Contributor Eight years of software development experience, specializing in software design, coding, testing, and debugging. Proficient in writing clean, efficient, and maintainable code using various programming languages and tools.

person.name: Emin Alemdar
person.bio: Emin is working as a Solutions Architect and has undertaken tasks such as producing solutions related to Kubernetes, Cloud technologies and Cloud Native Transformation in general, and transferring best practices about these areas. He is also an AWS Container Hero and shares his experience with the community around these technologies. He is part of the organiser team of the official CNCF Istanbul Community Chapter (https://community.cncf.io/istanbul/). He is also part of the CNCF Glossary Turkish team and helps the local community to understand the Cloud Native terms in their native language. He publishes blog posts from his personal Medium page (https://medium.com/@eminalemdar) and also participates in Open Source and Cloud Native related conferences and delivers talks about these technologies. He is in the organiser team of KCD (Kubernetes Community Days) Istanbul conference.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Community activities have always been my biggest motivation throughout my career. Helping people and learning from the people by participating in community activities is extremely important for me. Cloud Native Community is really one of a kind and being part of this community is really like a dream coming true for me.

person.name: Giridhar Sakineni
person.bio: I got interested in Kubernetes in 2019 when I started reading the book "Kubernetes in Action" by Marko Luksa and this is still the best book I ever read. Ever since then, my interest in Kubernetes started growing and last 4 years I have been working on Kubernetes platform engineering team for a large healthcare company focused on delivering Kubernetes Infrastructure to development teams. Prior to working on Kubernetes, I had extensive experience working on back-end development in C# .Net and MEAN stack and had experience developing and deploying micro-services to GCP/AWS

person.name: Lukasz Gryglicki
person.bio:
Lukasz has been a Senior Developer at the CNCF since 2017 and has been working for The Linux Foundation since 2018. He is the creator and maintainer of the DevStats reporting tool. Prior to joining the CNCF, Lukasz worked remotely for companies based in the US, including Cleverstep, Jamis and Spreemo Health.

He loves to travel in polar areas, such as polar Norway, Finland and Russia. From 2011 to 2012, he was a scientist for a Polish polar expedition to the Hornsund fjord on the island of Spitsbergen, in northern Norway. Lukasz graduated from Warsaw University of Technology with a Master of Science in Engineering. He lives in a small town in Poland with his wife and two kids.

person.name: Manoj K R
person.bio: I am a DevOps and Cloud Engineer specializing in Kubernetes, container security, and CNCF technologies, with experience managing self-hosted clusters as well as AWS, Azure, and GCP environments. I am an AWS Solutions Architect Associate and Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA), actively contributing to Kubernetes upgrades, security patching, and ingress/controller optimizations. I share insights through Meetups and community discussions on CNCF ecosystem tools like cert-manager, Cilium, Linkerd, and Kyverno, helping teams improve reliability and security. You can find more of my work and writings on LinkedIn and my technical blog.

person.name: Taylor Christie
person.bio: I'm a platform engineer that focuses on cloud efficiency and operational excellence. I specialize in open source technologies, cloud cost optimization, and improving operational success in the cloud - specifically for companies that need to scale aggressively. I work heavily with kubernetes, FluxCD and Istio but have a lot of experience with many other open source technologies as well.

person.name: Jānis Orlovs
person.bio: 15+ years of in IT operations, organising software delivery and security focusing on financial services. Enjoying well built self healing systems what makes life easier.  Time to time speaker in local conferences and meetups.

person.name: Yuki Nanjo
person.bio: Yuki Nanjo is a Cloud Solution Engineer, Application Developer, DevSecOps Engineer. Running Application on Kubernetes and Optimize CI/CD pipelines to accelerate innovations! My Blog here: https://namyusql.hatenablog.com About Kubernetes, Azure pipeline, and so on.

person.name: Brian Brookman
person.bio: I'm an experienced IT engineer who specializes in networking, cloud, and Kubernetes. By applying cloud native principles, engaging with the community, and contributing to open source, I help people build and operate scalable, resilient infrastructure both on-premises and in the cloud.

person.name: Saed Farah
person.bio: A Security Engineer at Google, with a strong passion for community collaboration. As a community leader I enjoy advocateing for all things security, cloud and containers.I run a weekly newsletter called: 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗘𝗻𝗴𝗶𝗻𝗲𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗪𝗲𝗲𝗸𝗹𝘆 - 𝗦𝗲𝗰𝘂𝗿𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗘𝗱𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻. I also enjoy creating videos where I demonstrate cutting edge tech containerised in an attempt to make knowledge more accessible to the community.

person.name: Tristan
person.bio: I like to do cool things with computers and anything related, doesn't matter if it runs on bare metal, the cloud or cars.

person.name: Pavel Luksha
person.bio: Systems and DevOps engineer with a strong focus on cloud technologies, infrastructure and the Kubernetes ecosystem. Experienced in designing, building, automating and securing cloud-native environments. Passionate about continuous learning, always eager to explore new tools and improve system reliability and security.

person.name: Romain Ballan
person.bio: I am a Senior Site Reliability Engineer working on cloud-native platforms with a cloud-agnostic mindset. I design, build, and operate reliable, scalable systems by applying modern engineering practices. I rely on industry standards, with a strong focus on long-term maintainability and operational quality.

person.name: Ahmed Safwat Mostafa
person.bio: Results-driven Senior DevOps Engineer with 10+ years of experience in cloud infrastructure, automation, and devops practices. Expertise in Kubernetes, cloud platforms, CICD, and Infrastructure as Code.

person.name: Khine Nyo Thant
person.bio: I am a DevOps Engineer with a developer background, now focusing on cloud-native technologies, Kubernetes, and cloud architecture. I specialize in building scalable, automated, and resilient infrastructure for modern applications. I am particularly interested in Platform Engineering and continuously explore ways to optimize cloud and Kubernetes environments.

person.name: Subash P
person.bio: I'm a Cloud and DevOps Architect with extensive experience in debugging and troubleshooting. Very keen on learning new things. Kubernetes always makes me curious due to it's elasticity which made to acquire Kubeastronaut.

person.name: Daniel D M
person.bio: Meet a Cloud Architect who writes code with the enthusiasm of a kid in a candy store. Armed with coffee and an insatiable appetite for automation, I transform mundane tasks into elegant solutions while Kubernetes keeps me humble with its delightful complexities.  As a devoted servant of Jesus Christ, I approach technology with the same dedication as my faith. When not wrestling with cloud infrastructure, you'll find me exploring new technologies and tools, always curious to learn what's next in the tech world. I embrace a minimalist lifestyle, finding joy in simplicity both in code and in life.  My love affair with Kubernetes stems from its beautiful chaos - where community-driven solutions tackle real-world problems head-on. Like a digital shepherd, I guide applications through the cloud, learning and growing alongside an amazing community of problem solvers.  Think of me as your friendly neighbourhood automation enthusiast, fueled by coffee and powered by faith, always ready to transform complex challenges into elegant solutions. Because in the end, technology is not just about code - it's about building bridges and growing together.  Life's journey isn't just about conquering technical challenges; it's about leaving footprints of kindness and knowledge for others to follow. After all, the greatest legacy we can leave behind is not in the systems we build, but in the lives we touch through sharing and caring.  Personal Blog: https://bookofdaniel.in/

person.name: Antonio Luzzi
person.bio: I am an IT Architecture Design Senior with more than 20 years of experience in enterprise system design and distributed architectures. I specialize in Kubernetes, DevOps automation, service mesh, observability, and secure cloud-native platforms, with hands-on experience running production clusters and large-scale public sector systems. Certified CKA, CKAD, and CKS, I am passionate about applying CNCF technologies to build resilient, scalable, and modern infrastructure platforms. I actively promote best practices in cloud-native architecture and continuous improvement across teams.

person.name: Zied
person.bio: I am a cloud-native technology enthusiast, specializing in Kubernetes solutions and CNCF projects. As a Kubestronaut, I regularly share my insights and knowledge through articles on my blog, engaging with the community on best practices and innovations in the field. My expertise includes managing Kubernetes clusters, automating deployments, and optimizing application performance. You can explore my contributions and read my articles at https://acegik.medium.com/

person.name: Leo Paillier
person.bio: Strong Cloud architect with a passion for innovation and a focus on DevSecOps. I bring extensive experience in cloud technologies, particularly Kubernetes and Observability. Currently working as an Enterprise Sales Engineer at Datadog where I help customers on their Observability journey. My motto: Always keep learning.

person.name: Simon Quatresols
person.bio: Simon is an experienced software and systems engineer who is grateful for the opportunity to work with Kubernetes and other CNCF projects every day. His key areas of interest include automation, site reliability and observability. He regularly attends local technology-related meetups, such as the CNCF Meetup Linz, and invests time daily to stay up-to-date with the latest developments in the open-source and cloud-native ecosystems.

person.name: Syed Asad Raza
person.bio: Hi Team, I am DevOps and MultiCloud Engineer and happy to become a CNCF member to add some input to this journey.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: It provides a platform to connect with other professionals in the industry, share knowledge and expertise, and contribute to the growth of the community. As an ambassador, one can learn about the latest developments and trends in the cloud-native ecosystem and play a role in shaping the future of the industry.

person.name: Emrah Sifoğlu
person.bio: My DevOps journey began with the question: "How do you check the logs of a running container on Kubernetes?" That conversation with infra engineer gave me a new perspective which led me to pursue DevOps. Now, I create CI pipelines on GitLab and automate tasks using Ansible. Becoming Kubestronaut is not only an achievement but also the beginning for me to deepen my knowledge.

person.name: Kārlis Akots Gribulis
person.bio: I am a Latvian container platform engineer with focus on building, maintaining and improving Kubernetes platforms and developer experience within them. Actively use and promote open source products within my workplaces. Have also made some minor open source contributions to products, such as, Grafana, Prometheus, etc.

person.name: Ankur Gupta
person.bio: With over 15 years of experience in IT, I began my journey in Linux OS customization and RPM packaging before moving into private cloud technologies like OpenStack and VMware. For the past 8 years, I've specialized in Kubernetes, working extensively with CNCF technologies across enterprise platforms such as Red Hat OpenShift and SUSE Rancher. I currently lead the Container Platform team at HPE, supporting global customers with Kubernetes, DevOps, and cloud-native solutions. To expand my capabilities in AI/ML-native workloads on Kubernetes, I recently completed my Master's in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning from Liverpool John Moores University (LJMU), UK.

person.name: Max Körbächer
person.bio: Max Körbächer is the Founder and Chief Technology Advisor at Liquid Reply. He is serving as a CNCF Ambassador, a member of the Linux Foundation Europe Advisory Board, and Founder and Emeritus Lead of the former CNCF Environmental Sustainability TAG.His expertise centers on Platform Engineering, which he uses to simplify complex systems in the cloud and AI space, a topic he details in his book, Platform Engineering for Architects. Max is also a dedicated community leader, notably organizing the Cloud Native Days (formerly KCD) in Munich & Ukraine, and the Kubernetes & Cloud Native Meetup in Munich.

person.name: Calum Murray
person.bio: I'm a Knative Eventing Maintainer and UX Working Group Lead. I co-founded the University of Toronto Open Source Students, where I help university students contribute to open source projects, such as Knative and CloudEvents.

person.name: Michael Sprott
person.bio: Michael is a seasoned DevSecOps Engineer and Senior Linux Administrator with over 8 years of experience specializing in infrastructure automation, system configuration, and mission-critical cyber defense. With a deep-seated passion for automating complex environments, he has spent his career optimizing enterprise systems and high-availability architectures. This drive for efficiency extends to his personal life, where he applies the same professional rigor to automating his homelab, managing sophisticated digital storage and backup solutions, and developing interactive study games to sharpen his technical edge.

person.name: Ivan Lenders
person.bio: I am an experienced IT consultant with a strong background in maintaining and upgrading production systems in the automotive sector. My expertise lies in enterprise Java development, system architecture, and cloud technologies. I hold CKA, CKAD and CKS certifications and am currently renewing my AWS Solution Architect Associate certification. I have a keen interest in Kubernetes, cloud computing, and modern software development practices, always looking to expand my knowledge and stay up to date with the latest technologies.

person.name: Sharique Ahmed
person.bio: I’m a DevOps engineer who lives in the cloud-native world, working with Kubernetes, AWS, and CNCF tools to build scalable and secure platforms. Most days you’ll find me automating something, fixing something, or breaking something just to learn from it. I enjoy experimenting with CNCF technologies and having fun while navigating the ever-expanding cloud-native galaxy.

person.name: Firas Ben Nacib
person.bio: Hi, I’m Firas, a Tunisian DevOps Engineer passionate about cloud-native technologies and the CNCF ecosystem. My focus is on building scalable, resilient platforms using Kubernetes, GitOps, and open source tooling, and day to day you’ll find me on Kubernetes, Terraform, and AWS. I’m excited to keep challenging myself, learning new things, and sharing my experience with everyone.

person.name: Matthias Brodbeck
person.bio: I'm passionate about open source and cloud-native technologies, focusing on modern container orchestration and infrastructure automation. My current interests include building secure, resilient, and scalable systems, while actively exploring best practices in DevOps and GitOps. I enjoy contributing to and learning from the CNCF community and regularly experiment with new approaches.

person.name: Anil Yadav
person.bio: Anil is enthusiast about Open Sources technology and believe in technology for everyone. Working with kubernetes for last 6 years.

person.name: Edgaras Apsega
person.bio: I am a passionate technologist with a focus on cloud-native technologies, particularly Kubernetes. As a co-organizer of Cloud Native Lithuania Meetup, I have helped to build a strong community of like-minded professionals where everyone is sharing their own experience. In addition to my meetup involvement, I am a speaker at technology conferences and currently work as an SRE at Vinted, where I bring my expertise in Kubernetes to help ensure the reliability and scalability of our systems.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm excited to be an ambassador because it enables me to connect with like-minded other ambassadors, share our knowledges and expertise and will help to educate and promote cloud-native technologies to a wider audience. The role also provides access to exclusive events and resources, as well as opportunities for professional growth and career development. Overall, being a Cloud Native Foundation Ambassador is a great way to give back to the community!

person.name: Mohamed Shibli Alfathih
person.bio: Senior DevOps / Site Reliability Engineer with 10+ years of experience designing and operating highly available cloud platforms across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
person.location: Al Warqaa 1, Dubai, United Arab Emirates

person.name: Dimitrios Vasilopoulos
person.bio: Passionate Cloud & Container Security Architect, focused on securing cloud-native ecosystems, driving secure DevOps, and empowering businesses to innovate safely and confidently in the cloud — at scale.

person.name: Håkon Solbjørg
person.bio: Håkon has a background in software development and an interest for DevOps. Automation, CI/CD, reproducible builds and pipelines - anything to make the builds and deploys of applications to go smooth, and when they don't, there is a reproducible build process to debug locally. It all started on linux servers before ascending to the clouds. As a software developer at heart, Håkon sees the deployment pipeline from code to running in production.
